channel.name: AI Dev Tools Directory
channel.subject: Suggested AI Dev Tools listing: Ralph Workflow

submitted_payload.suggested_entry: Ralph Workflow — free and open-source workflow layer for autonomous coding that composes planning, implementation, and verification into a reviewable repo-native flow.

why_this_action: Shared artifacts still show distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the live bottleneck, the highest-priority need is still Codeberg-first qualified discovery rather than more owned content, and AI Dev Tools is a fresh developer-focused directory that matches Ralph Workflow's product shape unusually well. Its public submit page exposed a broken placeholder Formspree endpoint from this runtime, but the site also exposed a real editor contact address, so direct curator outreach was the strongest legitimate same-run fallback instead of abandoning the lane.
shared_findings_reused: MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md: prioritize Codeberg-first distribution and proof-led positioning
FOUR_MARKETING_QUESTIONS.md: preserved what it is, who it is for, why different, why now
ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: keep pushing proof-led free-use conversion instead of more generic posting
market_intelligence_latest.json: reused canonical positioning truths and category fit
adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg is still flat, so fresh qualified discovery lanes remain the priority
outreach-log.md: verified AI Dev Tools had not already been contacted

verification.broken_form_result: HTTP 404 from the public submit form action

measurement_window.success_signal: reply, listing inclusion on AI Dev Tools, or a live third-party citation/backlink pointing evaluators to the Codeberg primary repo
measurement_window.kill_condition: If no reply or listing signal arrives by 2026-06-06, replace this lane with a different untouched developer-tool curator surface instead of resending the same pitch.
